deep_dive.title: Learning When to Denoise: Optimizing Asynchronous Schedules for Latent Diffusion
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-representation diffusion models can improve visual synthesis by denoising complementary views of an image, but their performance depends critically on the asynchronous schedule that determines when each representation is denoised. We propose to learn this schedule. Our method formulates asynchronous flow matching over multiple representation spaces and uses a schedule-corrected objective that keeps each representation's local noising-time weights fixed as the schedule changes. We instantiate the schedule with a flexible parametric class that is convex and monotone by construction, and learn it using a fast joint probe with less than 1% additional training compute. On ImageNet 256x256, the learned schedule substantially improves both convergence speed and final quality under a matched 675M-parameter XL backbone. With AutoGuidance, our 200-epoch model reaches FID 1.05, matching the 800-epoch SFD-XL baseline with 4x less training. Training to 600 epochs further improves to FID 1.02, outperforming the 1B-parameter SFD-XXL result of FID 1.04 while using a smaller model. In the unguided setting, our 200-epoch model reaches FID 2.37, already below the best 800-epoch SFD-XL result (2.54) at 4x less training, and improves to FID 2.14 at 600 epochs. Code is available at https://github.com/bsq532087/LWD
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: directly improves latent diffusion models by learning denoising schedules
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 直接改进潜在扩散模型通过学习去噪调度
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 直接改进潜在扩散模型通过学习去噪调度
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 直接改进潜在扩散模型通过学习去噪调度
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes learning asynchronous denoising schedules for multi-representation latent diffusion improving both fidelity and efficiency with minimal compute.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多表示潜在扩散模型的性能高度依赖异步去噪调度，但手工设计调度费时且难最优。本文提出可学习调度方法，通过将异步流匹配公式化并引入调度修正目标，在仅增加不到1%训练计算量的情况下自动学习最优凸单调调度。在ImageNet 256x256上，该方法在提升生成质量的同时保持了采样效率，为扩散模型在视频压缩等应用中的高效部署提供了关键工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多表示潜在扩散模型的性能高度依赖异步去噪调度，但手工设计调度费时且难最优。本文提出可学习调度方法，通过将异步流匹配公式化并引入调度修正目标，在仅增加不到1%训练计算量的情况下自动学习最优凸单调调度。在ImageNet 256x256上，该方法在提升生成质量的同时保持了采样效率，为扩散模型在视频压缩等应用中的高效部署提供了关键工具。

quick_skim.title: MoECa: Aligning Feature Reuse with Expert Decomposition in Diffusion Transformers
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion Transformers with Mixture-of-Experts (DiT-MoE) improve model capacity under sparse activation, but diffusion inference is still bottlenecked by redundant computation across timesteps. Existing caching methods mainly operate at the token level, which becomes suboptimal in DiT-MoE because each token update is internally decomposed into multiple routed expert branches. Our analysis shows that cross-timestep redundancy in DiT-MoE is better characterized at the expert-branch level than at the whole-token level. Based on this observation, we propose MoECa, a fine-grained caching framework that performs branch-level feature reuse across timesteps. MoECa further introduces expert-aware adaptive control and synchronized cache updates across MoE and attention paths to maintain stable intermediate states. Experiments on multiple DiT-MoE models show that MoECa consistently achieves a better speed-quality trade-off than prior caching methods, with up to 2.83$\times$ inference speedup and minimal quality degradation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 潜在扩散模型特征复用
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 潜在扩散模型特征复用
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 潜在扩散模型特征复用
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MoECa proposes a fine-grained caching framework for Diffusion Transformers with Mixture-of-Experts, achieving branch-level feature reuse across timesteps to reduce redundant computation. It introduces expert-aware adaptive control and synchronized cache updates. Experiments show inference speedup without quality loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散Transformer虽然通过混合专家（MoE）提升了模型容量，但跨时间步的计算冗余仍然严重。现有缓存方法在令牌级别操作，不适应MoE的专家分支分解。本文提出MoECa，一种细粒度缓存框架，在专家分支级别实现跨时间步的特征复用，并引入专家感知的自适应控制和同步缓存更新。实验表明，该方法在不损失生成质量的前提下显著加速扩散推理，为扩散模型在视频压缩等生成任务中的高效部署提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 扩散Transformer虽然通过混合专家（MoE）提升了模型容量，但跨时间步的计算冗余仍然严重。现有缓存方法在令牌级别操作，不适应MoE的专家分支分解。本文提出MoECa，一种细粒度缓存框架，在专家分支级别实现跨时间步的特征复用，并引入专家感知的自适应控制和同步缓存更新。实验表明，该方法在不损失生成质量的前提下显著加速扩散推理，为扩散模型在视频压缩等生成任务中的高效部署提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Mix-QVLA: Task-Evidence-Aware Mixed-Precision Quantization of Vision-Language-Action Models
quick_skim.abstract: We propose Mix-QVLA, a task-evidence-aware mixed-precision PTQ framework for VLA models. Mix-QVLA anchors each quantized variant to the full-precision action-token reference decision and evaluates whether quantization preserves task-relevant evidence across key VLA functional boundaries. It computes normalized gradient-weighted task-evidence maps from boundary activations and compares full-precision and quantized maps using evidence-mass and attribution-distribution distortion, capturing changes in both the strength and allocation of decision-supporting evidence. A soft-bottleneck objective aggregates boundary-level degradation into layer-wise sensitivity scores. Mix-QVLA further models sensitivity throughout task execution, capturing phase-dependent shifts in layer importance rather than assuming a fixed sensitivity profile. The resulting evidence- and time-aware scores guide mixed-precision bit allocation under model-size and BitOps budgets. Extensive evaluations on OpenVLA-style policies show that Mix-QVLA improves the accuracy-efficiency trade-off of low-bit VLA deployment. On LIBERO, Mix-QVLA reduces OpenVLA-OFT memory from 15.4 GB to 4.1 GB, retains 96.3 average success compared with 97.1 for the BF16 model, and achieves a 1.52x inference speedup.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: mixed-precision quantization aware training for latent representations
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对潜在表示的混合精度量化感知训练
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对潜在表示的混合精度量化感知训练
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对潜在表示的混合精度量化感知训练
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes task-evidence-aware mixed-precision quantization for VLA models, analyzing layer-wise sensitivity with task evidence maps.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出任务证据感知的混合精度量化框架Mix-QVLA，用于视觉-语言-动作模型的低成本部署。该方法通过计算梯度加权任务证据图，量化全精度与量化模型之间的证据质量与分布差异，从而识别关键敏感层并分配混合精度。虽然面向VLA模型，但其任务感知的量化训练策略与可微分量化思路直接适用于视频压缩中潜在表示的量化优化，可提升低复杂度视频编解码器的率失真性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出任务证据感知的混合精度量化框架Mix-QVLA，用于视觉-语言-动作模型的低成本部署。该方法通过计算梯度加权任务证据图，量化全精度与量化模型之间的证据质量与分布差异，从而识别关键敏感层并分配混合精度。虽然面向VLA模型，但其任务感知的量化训练策略与可微分量化思路直接适用于视频压缩中潜在表示的量化优化，可提升低复杂度视频编解码器的率失真性能。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Differentiable quantization optimization for latent in video compression

quick_skim.title: Variable-Length Tokenization via Learnable Global Merging for Diffusion Transformers
quick_skim.abstract: Latent Diffusion Models (LDMs) have become dominant in visual synthesis, but their quality-compute trade-off is largely constrained by the tokenizer's fixed compression ratio. Variable-length tokenizers (VLTs) promise adaptive compression by varying token counts, allowing diffusion models to flexibly balance quality and compute. However, conventional VLTs modulate length by truncating ordered token sequences, which makes token semantics depend on token position and breaks representational alignment across lengths. This leads to a cross-length shift in the latent distribution that hinders a single variable-length diffusion model from operating effectively. To address this, we propose a novel variable-length tokenizer that modulates length by merging tokens. We show that encouraging similar tokens to merge enables direct cross-length representation alignment when the diffusion transformer operates according to the merging pattern. Since conventional merging methods are data-dependent, making the merging pattern inaccessible during generation, we introduce learnable global merging, which is data-independent, to ensure compatibility with diffusion transformers. On ImageNet 256$\times$256 generation, our merging-based variable-length tokenizer integrated with a diffusion transformer achieves a superior gFID-compute trade-off compared to prior VLT methods. Code is available at [this https URL](https://github.com/movinghoon/lgm)
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Novel variable-length tokenizer for latent diffusion models; addresses token count adaptation for quality-compute trade-off, relevant to latent diffusion in video compression
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向潜在扩散模型的可变长度标记化方法，通过自适应调整token数量平衡质量与计算量，与视频编码中潜在扩散技术相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向潜在扩散模型的可变长度标记化方法，通过自适应调整token数量平衡质量与计算量，与视频编码中潜在扩散技术相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向潜在扩散模型的可变长度标记化方法，通过自适应调整token数量平衡质量与计算量，与视频编码中潜在扩散技术相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a learnable global merging tokenizer that produces variable-length token sequences for diffusion transformers, enabling adaptive compression and better quality-compute trade-off for LDMs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有潜在扩散模型受限于固定压缩率的问题，本文提出一种可学习全局合并的可变长度标记化方法，使扩散模型能动态调整token数量以灵活平衡质量与计算量。实验表明该方法有效消除跨长度分布偏移，允许单一扩散模型在不同质量等级下运行。该技术可扩展至视频潜在扩散编码器，为生成式视频压缩提供新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有潜在扩散模型受限于固定压缩率的问题，本文提出一种可学习全局合并的可变长度标记化方法，使扩散模型能动态调整token数量以灵活平衡质量与计算量。实验表明该方法有效消除跨长度分布偏移，允许单一扩散模型在不同质量等级下运行。该技术可扩展至视频潜在扩散编码器，为生成式视频压缩提供新思路。
